assertions.assertion: Sets RequireDPoP = true on the client configuration
assertions.rationale: General OAuth knowledge would lead to suggesting a RequireDPoP-like property, and this is documented in public DPoP specs.

assertions.assertion: Sets DPoPClockSkew to TimeSpan.FromMinutes(2) on the client
assertions.rationale: The specific property name DPoPClockSkew on the Duende Client model is not general knowledge; an LLM might guess a different configuration approach.

assertions.assertion: Explains that DPoP binds the token to the client's key via the cnf claim with a jkt (JWK thumbprint) value
assertions.rationale: DPoP RFC 9449 is public knowledge; the cnf/jkt mechanism is well-documented in the standard.

assertions.assertion: Mentions that DPoP requires Enterprise Edition and version >= 6.3
assertions.rationale: Edition requirements are Duende-specific licensing details not available in general knowledge.

assertions.assertion: Client still uses AllowedGrantTypes = GrantTypes.ClientCredentials with a shared secret, demonstrating DPoP is separate from client authentication
assertions.rationale: The DPoP RFC makes clear that DPoP is orthogonal to client authentication, general knowledge covers this.
